memberdef.name: blend_poisson_bf

briefdescription.para: Blends two arrays using Poisson blending with a brute-force solver.

detaileddescription.para: This function performs Poisson blending between array1 and array2 over a specified number of iterations. Optionally, a mask can be provided to control the blending regions.

parameterdescription.para: The first input array.
The second input array.
The number of iterations for the blending process (default: 500).
Optional pointer to an array defining the blending mask. If null, blending is applied globally.

simplesect.para: The blended array resulting from the Poisson blending operation.

detaileddescription.para: Applies a smoothing/deposition pass that fills local depressions while preserving overall terrain shape through gradient-aware blending.

Influence radius of the Gaussian filter.
Blending factor controlling deposition intensity.
Number of successive deposition passes.

memberdef.name: hydraulic_particle

briefdescription.para: Simulates hydraulic erosion on a heightmap using particle-based flow.

detaileddescription.para: Particles traverse the heightmap z, eroding and depositing material according to local slope, capacity, inertia, and optional directional bias.

parameterdescription.para: Heightmap array to modify.
Number of erosion particles to simulate.
Random seed for particle initialization.
Optional bedrock array to limit erosion.
Optional moisture map affecting erosion/deposition.
Optional elevation shift map.
Optional output array recording total erosion.
Optional output array recording deposition.
Sediment capacity of each particle.
Erosion rate coefficient.
Deposition rate coefficient.
Particle inertia factor.
Gravity effect on particle movement.
Particle velocity damping.
Sediment evaporation rate.
Enable bias along a preferred slope direction.
Bias angle in degrees (if directional bias enabled).

briefdescription.para: See hmap::hydraulic_particle.

memberdef.name: hydraulic_procedural

briefdescription.para: Apply phase-guided hydraulic procedural erosion to a heightmap.

detaileddescription.para: Computes a phase field from the input terrain and generates ridge-aligned erosion patterns modulated by flow accumulation and local gradient. Optionally applies procedural noise, deposition, and outputs a ridge mask.

parameterdescription.para: Heightmap to erode (modified in place).
Global kernel size controlling feature scale.
Global erosion intensity.
Random seed for phase/noise generation.
Ridge shaping profile type.
Parameter controlling the profile shape.
Global phase angle offset (degrees).
Controls smoothing between phase transitions.
Reference talus angle for flow accumulation.
Blend factor for gradient-based modulation.
Exponent applied to normalized gradient.
If true, applies post-erosion deposition pass.
If true, generates internal FBM noise fields.
Amplitude of procedural noise.
Optional spatial modulation of kernel size.
Optional spatial phase shift.
Optional external noise field (x component).
Optional external noise field (y component).
Optional output ridge mask (cosine of phase).
World-space bounding box.

memberdef.argsstring: (Array &z, float kp_global, float c_erosion, uint seed, ErosionProfile erosion_profile=ErosionProfile::EP_TRIANGLE_GRENIER, int octaves=3, float persistence=0.5f, float lacunarity=2.f, float erosion_profile_parameter=0.01f, float angle_shift=0.f, float phase_smoothing=0.1f, float talus_ref=0.001f, float gradient_scaling_ratio=1.f, float gradient_power=0.8f, bool exclude_ridges=true, bool apply_deposition=false, float deposition_strength=1.f, bool enable_default_noise=true, float noise_amp=0.01f, const Array *p_kp_multiplier=nullptr, const Array *p_angle_shift=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, Array *p_ridge_mask=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})

detaileddescription.para: Applies the erosion operator across multiple octaves with lacunarity/persistence scaling, accumulating a normalized ridge mask.

parameterdescription.para: Heightmap to erode (modified in place).
Base kernel size.
Base erosion intensity.
Random seed.
Ridge shaping profile.
Number of fBm octaves.
Amplitude decay per octave.
Frequency growth per octave.
Profile parameter.
Global phase shift (degrees).
Phase smoothing factor.
Reference talus angle.
Gradient modulation ratio.
Gradient exponent.
Enable deposition pass.
Enable internal noise generation.
Noise amplitude.
Optional spatial kernel modulation.
Optional spatial phase shift.
Optional external noise (x).
Optional external noise (y).
Optional accumulated ridge mask output.
World-space bounding box.

memberdef.name: hydraulic_schott

briefdescription.para: Simulates hydraulic erosion and deposition on a heightmap using the Schott method.

detaileddescription.para: This function performs hydraulic erosion on the given heightmap z over a specified number of iterations. It includes parameters for controlling erosion, deposition, and flow routing. Optional flow accumulation can also be computed and stored in the p_flow array.

parameterdescription.para: The heightmap array to be modified. Heights are updated in-place.
The number of iterations for the hydraulic erosion process.
An array defining the slope threshold for erosion.
Erosion coefficient (default: 1.0f).
Thermal erosion coefficient (default: 0.1f).
Deposition coefficient (default: 0.2f).
Exponent controlling the influence of flow accumulation on erosion (default: 0.8f).
Exponent controlling the influence of flow accumulation on deposition (default: 0.8f).
Exponent controlling flow routing behavior (default: 1.3f).
Weight of thermal erosion effects (default: 1.5f).
Weight of deposition effects (default: 2.5f).
Optional pointer to an array for storing flow accumulation data. If null, flow data is not returned (default: nullptr).

simplesect.para: Taken from https://hal.science/hal-04565030v1/document
Only available if OpenCL is enabled.

memberdef.argsstring: (Array &z, int iterations, const Array &talus, Array *p_mask, float c_erosion=1.f, float c_thermal=0.1f, float c_deposition=0.2f, float flow_acc_exponent=0.8f, float flow_acc_exponent_depo=0.8f, float flow_routing_exponent=1.3f, float thermal_weight=1.5f, float deposition_weight=2.5f, Array *p_flow=nullptr)

memberdef.name: mudslide

briefdescription.para: Simulate a mudslide (landslide-driven material redistribution) on a height field.

detaileddescription.para: Applies an iterative viscous flow process to the height array z, constrained by landslide_mask and limited by the specified depth. The flow behavior is controlled by a depth exponent and a viscosity power law. Optionally outputs the transported depth map.

parameterdescription.para: Input/output height field modified in place.
Mask defining affected areas (non-zero = active).
Maximum erosion/deposition depth.
Number of simulation iterations.
Exponent applied to depth influence (default 0.5f).
Power exponent controlling viscosity response (default 1.5f).
Optional pointer to store resulting depth map (nullable).

briefdescription.para: See hmap::mudslide.

memberdef.name: rifts

briefdescription.para: Applies a "rift" deformation effect to a heightmap array.

detaileddescription.para: This function modifies the given heightmap by introducing linear or radial "rift-like" noise patterns. The deformation can be controlled by several parameters such as direction, amplitude, noise shifts, and optional external noise arrays. Optionally, the effect can be masked using a power-based blending function.

parameterdescription.para: Reference to the heightmap array to be modified in-place.
Frequency vector (kx, ky) scaling the deformation in X and Y directions.
Orientation of the rift in degrees (0° = horizontal, 90° = vertical).
Strength of the rift deformation applied to the heightmap.
Random seed used for deterministic noise generation.
Vertical offset applied to the base noise to shift elevation influence.
Lower smoothing factor for Voronoi-based noise computation.
Upper smoothing factor for Voronoi-based noise computation.
Amplitude controlling radial spreading away from the rift axis.
Amplitude scaling the influence of the heightmap's initial values as noise input.
Minimum clamp value for the Voronoi noise before remapping.
Minimum remap value for scaling noise output.
If true, applies a power-based blending mask instead of a direct overwrite.
Gamma exponent used when applying the mask to control blending.
Optional pointer to an external noise array for X-offset perturbation (nullptr if unused).
Optional pointer to an external noise array for Y-offset perturbation (nullptr if unused).
2D vector specifying the central point around which the rift effect is computed.
Bounding box (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) defining the spatial domain of the heightmap.

memberdef.argsstring: (Array &z, Array *p_mask, const Array &talus, Array *p_deposition_map=nullptr, float max_deposition=0.01, int iterations=5, int thermal_subiterations=10)

parameterdescription.para: Input array.
Intensity mask, expected in [0, 1] (applied as a post-processing).
Talus limit.
[out] Reference to the deposition map, provided as an output field.
Maximum height of sediment deposition.
Number of iterations.
Number of thermal erosion iterations for each pass.

memberdef.name: sediment_layer

briefdescription.para: Applies a talus-based sediment deposition layer.

detaileddescription.para: Computes a sediment (deposition) map using thermal erosion constrained by per-cell talus limits. Optionally smooths the result and blends it back into the heightmap.

parameterdescription.para: Heightmap to modify.
Base talus values for erosion.
Upper talus threshold used for masking.
Number of erosion iterations.
Enable post smoothing and blending.
Optional output deposition map (may be null).

memberdef.argsstring: (Array &z, float angle, float slope, float gamma, uint seed, bool linear_gamma=true, float kz=1.f, int octaves=4, float lacunarity=2.f, float gamma_noise_ratio=0.5f, float noise_amp=0.4f, const glm::vec2 &noise_kw={4.f, 4.f}, bool enable_ridge_noise=true, const glm::vec2 &ridge_noise_kw={4.f, 1.2f}, float ridge_angle_shift=45.f, float ridge_noise_amp=0.5f, float ridge_clamp_vmin=0.f, float ridge_remap_vmin=0.f, bool apply_elevation_mask=true, bool apply_ridge_mask=true, float mask_gamma=0.4f, const Array *p_mask=nullptr, const glm::vec4 &bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: strata

briefdescription.para: Applies stratification to a heightfield using directional noise and multiscale gamma transformations.

detaileddescription.para: This function modifies the input heightfield z by simulating geological strata patterns. It combines directional shifts, fractal noise, and ridge-based perturbations to produce layered structures in the data. The MUST BE NORMALIZED in [0, 1].

parameterdescription.para: Reference to the heightfield array to modify, MUST BE NORMALIZED in [0, 1].
Horizontal orientation of the strata in degrees.
Vertical slope of the strata.
Gamma exponent for the non-linear remapping (e.g., 0.5 for smoothing, 1.5 for sharpening).
Seed for deterministic noise generation.
If true, applies sharp linear gamma mapping; if false, uses smooth gamma mapping.
Base scaling factor for the stratification frequency.
Number of iterative stratification passes (multiscale detail).
Frequency multiplier applied at each octave for fractal scaling.
Ratio controlling how noise influences gamma variation (0 = no noise, 1 = full influence).
Amplitude of the base Perlin noise used to modulate the strata.
Frequency vector for the base Perlin noise along X and Y axes.
Frequency vector for the Voronoi ridge noise (x = main frequency, y = directional frequency).
Additional angular shift (in degrees) for the ridge direction, relative to angle.
Amplitude of the ridge noise modulation.
Minimum clamp value for ridge noise response.
Minimum remap value for ridge modulation (used for reverse remapping).
If true, applied an elevation mask on the effect.
Gamma applied to the mask used for blending original and stratified values for the elevation mask.
Optional filter mask, expected in the range [0, 1].
Bounding box of the domain as {xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax}.

listitem.para: Setting linear_gamma to false produces smoother transitions, while true creates sharper layer boundaries.
Increasing octaves adds multiscale detail but also increases computational cost.

memberdef.argsstring: (Array &z, glm::vec2 kw, float amp, uint seed, float gamma=0.5f, float gamma_lateral=0.4f, float angle=0.f, float noise_amp=0.5f, bool absolute_displacement=true, float occurence_probability=0.5f, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})

Use absolute instead of relative displacement.
Probability of cell activation.

Domain bounding box.

memberdef.name: thermal_scree

briefdescription.para: Performs thermal scree erosion on a heightmap.

detaileddescription.para: This function applies a thermal erosion process that redistributes material from steeper slopes to flatter areas, simulating talus formation. The process iterates a given number of times to achieve a more stable terrain profile.

parameterdescription.para: The heightmap to be modified in-place by the erosion process.
The threshold slope angles that determine where material is moved.
The maximum allowed elevation for erosion effects.
The number of erosion iterations to apply (default: 10).
Optional pointer to an array that stores the deposited material per cell.

memberdef.argsstring: (Array &z, const Array *p_mask, const Array &talus, const Array &zmax, int iterations=10, Array *p_deposition_map=nullptr)
memberdef.name: thermal_scree

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

memberdef.argsstring: (Array &z, const Array &talus, int iterations=100, float gamma=2.f, float ratio=0.8f, float zmin=0.f, float zmax=0.f, float elevation_max_ratio=1.f, bool preserve_elevation_range=true, const Array *p_noise=nullptr, Array *p_deposition_map=nullptr)
memberdef.name: valley_fill

briefdescription.para: Fill valleys using thermal scree deposition and height-based blending.

detaileddescription.para: Applies an erosion-based fill to valleys, then blends the result with the original heightmap using a gamma-shaped mask derived from elevation.

parameterdescription.para: Heightmap to modify in place.
Talus angle map controlling scree deposition.
Number of erosion iterations.
Gamma applied to the height-based mixing mask.
Blend ratio controlling valley influence.
Minimum height for normalization (auto if zmax <= zmin).
Maximum height for normalization (auto if zmax <= zmin).

memberdef.argsstring: (Array &z, const Array *p_mask, const Array &talus, int iterations=100, float gamma=2.f, float ratio=0.8f, float zmin=0.f, float zmax=0.f, float elevation_max_ratio=1.f, bool preserve_elevation_range=true, const Array *p_noise=nullptr, Array *p_deposition_map=nullptr)
memberdef.name: valley_fill

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &z, float amplitude=0.2f, float width=32.f, float edt_exponent=0.5f, int prefilter_ir=0, FlowDirectionMethod fd_method=FlowDirectionMethod::FDM_D8, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, const Array *p_scaling=nullptr)
memberdef.name: watershed_ridge

briefdescription.para: Carves watershed ridges using basin-wise distance transforms.

detaileddescription.para: Drainage basins are computed using D8 flow routing. For each basin, a distance transform to the basin boundary is evaluated and used to lower elevations near watershed divides, forming ridge lines.

parameterdescription.para: Input elevation field.
Ridge carving strength.
Smoothing.
Exponent applied to the distance field to control ridge sharpness.

simplesect.para: Elevation field with watershed ridges emphasized.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &z, const Array *p_mask, float amplitude=0.2f, float width=32.f, float edt_exponent=0.5f, int prefilter_ir=0, FlowDirectionMethod fd_method=FlowDirectionMethod::FDM_D8, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, const Array *p_scaling=nullptr)
memberdef.name: watershed_ridge

memberdef.name: bilateral_filter

briefdescription.para: Apply a bilateral filter to an array.

detaileddescription.para: This function performs edge-preserving smoothing by combining a spatial kernel (2D) and a value-domain kernel (1D). The spatial kernel controls the influence of neighboring samples based on their position, while the value kernel weights samples according to their value difference.

parameterdescription.para: Input array to be filtered.
2D spatial kernel defining neighborhood weights.
1D kernel applied in the value domain.
Scaling factor applied to value differences before sampling the 1D kernel.

simplesect.para: A new array containing the filtered result.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &array, int ir, float kernel1d_value_scaling)
memberdef.name: bilateral_filter

briefdescription.para: Apply a bilateral filter to an array using a radius-based Gaussian kernel.

detaileddescription.para: This overload builds the spatial kernel implicitly from the given radius. The value-domain kernel behavior is controlled by the same scaling factor as the main overload.

array
Input array to be filtered.
ir
Radius of the spatial neighborhood.
kernel1d_value_scaling
Scaling factor applied to value differences before computing value-domain weights.
A new array containing the filtered result.

memberdef.name: project_talus_along_direction

briefdescription.para: Projects array values along a given direction using talus attenuation.

detaileddescription.para: Applies a directional propagation (D8 convention) where values decay with distance according to a talus coefficient. The computation is performed on the GPU using an OpenCL kernel.

parameterdescription.para: Input 2D array.
Talus coefficient controlling distance-based attenuation.
Propagation direction (D8 convention, range [0–7]).

simplesect.para: A new Array containing the projected values.
The array is temporarily shifted to non-negative values to ensure correctness of OpenCL atomic operations on floats.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &array, float talus, const Array *p_mask, int direction=0)

memberdef.name: flow_accumulation_from_velocity_field

briefdescription.para: Approximates flow accumulation from a 2D velocity field.

detaileddescription.para: Advects a scalar accumulation field using an Eulerian transport scheme driven by the velocity components (u, v). Repeated iterations approximate upstream contributing area and highlight flow convergence (thalwegs).

parameterdescription.para: X-component of the flow velocity field.
Y-component of the flow velocity field.
Number of transport iterations to perform.

simplesect.para: Flow accumulation field.

memberdef.name: flow_simulation

briefdescription.para: GPU hydraulic flow simulation using a virtual-pipes model; simulates shallow-water transport over a height field using iterative flux computation and water transport passes, with optional flux diffusion and post-simulation depth clamping to remove thin residual water layers.

parameterdescription.para: Terrain height field.
Global water scaling factor.
Initial relative water distribution.
Number of simulation steps.
Time step size.
Enables flux smoothing.
Strength of flux diffusion.
Ratio for removing thin remaining water layers.

simplesect.para: Array Final simulated water depth map.

memberdef.name: snow_simulation

briefdescription.para: Simulate snow accumulation and redistribution over a terrain.

detaileddescription.para: Models snow fall, depth-dependent avalanching (above repose angle) and thermal creep smoothing (below repose angle), with optional melting and post-filtering. Snow progressively stabilizes into smooth, rounded forms.

parameterdescription.para: Heightfield of the underlying terrain.
Total amount of snow to deposit.
Spatial modulation of snow deposition.
Spatial melting factor in [0,1].
Local repose angle (talus slope).
Number of simulation steps.
Time step.
Fraction of iterations used for snowfall.
Avalanche transport coefficient.
Thermal creep (sub-repose smoothing) coefficient.
Global melting strength.
Controls snow stiffening with depth.
Depth influence on repose angle.
Apply final smoothing pass if true.
Controls creep strength relative to talus.

simplesect.para: Final snow depth field.

memberdef.name: local_relief

briefdescription.para: Compute the local relief of an array.

detaileddescription.para: The local relief is defined as the difference between the maximum and minimum values within a neighborhood of radius ir around each element. This provides a measure of local variation in the array (e.g., terrain ruggedness).

parameterdescription.para: Input array representing scalar values (e.g., elevation map).
Radius of the neighborhood (in pixels) used to compute local extrema.

simplesect.para: Array An array of the same size as array, where each element contains the difference between the local maximum and minimum within the specified neighborhood.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &array, int ir)
memberdef.name: local_variance

briefdescription.para: Compute the average local variance of an array.

detaileddescription.para: Local variance is defined as the standard deviation within a window of radius ir centered on each cell. The function computes this value for every cell, then returns the average over the entire domain.

parameterdescription.para: Input array.
Radius of the local window.

simplesect.para: Array containing the averaged local variance.

memberdef.name: badlands

briefdescription.para: Generates a synthetic "badlands" terrain heightmap.

detaileddescription.para: This function procedurally creates a 2D elevation field resembling badlands (highly eroded terrain with sharp ridges and valleys). It combines fractal noise (FBM) with a Voronoi-based primitive, displaced along a specified orientation.

parameterdescription.para: Output array shape (resolution in x and y).
Frequency vector controlling the scale of the features.
Random seed used for noise and Voronoi generation.
Controls roughness of the fractal noise (higher = more irregular).
Orientation angle (degrees) of terrain displacements.
Voronoi smoothing parameter (controls ridge sharpness).
Amplitude of the base displacement noise.
Optional pointer to external displacement noise field (X-axis).
Optional pointer to external displacement noise field (Y-axis).
Bounding box of the generation domain in normalized coordinates.

simplesect.para: Array containing the generated badlands heightmap.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, glm::vec2 kw, uint seed, float warp_kw=4.f, float large_scale_warp_amp=0.2f, float large_scale_gain=6.f, float large_scale_amp=0.2f, float medium_scale_kw_ratio=3.f, float medium_scale_warp_amp=1.f, float medium_scale_gain=7.f, float medium_scale_amp=0.08f, float small_scale_kw_ratio=10.f, float small_scale_amp=0.1f, float small_scale_overlay_amp=0.002f, float rugosity_kw_ratio=1.f, float rugosity_amp=1.f, bool flatten_activate=true, float flatten_kw_ratio=1.f, float flatten_amp=0.f, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: basalt_field

briefdescription.para: Generates a synthetic procedural terrain resembling basaltic landforms.

detaileddescription.para: This function creates a multi-scale procedural field combining large, medium, and small-scale Voronoi-based patterns, noise warping, and optional flattening, simulating the morphology of fractured basalt or volcanic terrains. The terrain is constructed using a combination of Voronoi diagrams (via voronoi_fbm) and fractal noise (noise_fbm), layered with frequency-domain manipulations and amplitude/gain controls at each scale.

The final output is a heightmap represented as an Array, normalized and composed of:
Large-scale cellular patterns with smoothed Voronoi edge distances.
Medium and small-scale structures introducing finer surface variation.
Optional rugosity (fine detail) and flattening to simulate erosion or flow effects.

parameterdescription.para: Output resolution (width x height) of the field.
Base wave numbers (frequency) for the terrain features.
Initial seed used for deterministic random generation.
Frequency of the warping noise that displaces Voronoi positions.
Amplitude of displacement for large-scale Voronoi warping.
Gain adjustment applied to the large-scale features.
Final amplitude of the large-scale height contribution.
Scaling factor for the frequency of the medium-scale patterns.
Amplitude of warping for the medium-scale displacement.
Gain control for medium-scale modulation.
Amplitude of the medium-scale heightmap.
Frequency ratio for small-scale details.
Amplitude of small-scale pattern contribution.
Additional overlay strength for repeating the small-scale pattern.
Frequency ratio for high-frequency noise applied as fine roughness.
Strength of the rugosity (high-frequency modulation).
Enables or disables the final flattening operation.
Frequency scaling of the flattening noise field.
Amplitude control of the flattening operation.
Optional pointer to a noise field used to displace grid coordinates in X.
Optional pointer to a noise field used to displace grid coordinates in Y.
The 2D bounding box ({xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax}) over which the terrain is generated.

simplesect.para: A procedurally generated Array representing the synthetic basalt-like terrain field.

listitem.para: This function relies on OpenCL-based kernels via the gpu:: namespace.
The returned field is normalized in amplitude but may require rescaling to match specific physical units.
Adjusting seed, warp_kw, and the gain/amplitude values can produce a wide variety of terrain features.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, glm::vec2 kw, uint seed, const Array &angle, float angle_spread_ratio=1.f, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})

memberdef.name: gavoronoise

briefdescription.para: Generates a 2D array using the GavoroNoise algorithm, which is a procedural noise technique for terrain generation and other applications.

detaileddescription.para:
shape
Dimensions of the output array.
kw
Wave number vector controlling the noise frequency.
seed
Seed value for random number generation.
amplitude
Amplitude of the noise.
kw_multiplier
Multiplier for wave numbers in the noise function.
slope_strength
Strength of slope-based directional erosion in the noise.
branch_strength
Strength of branch-like structures in the generated noise.
z_cut_min
Minimum cutoff for Z-value in the noise.
z_cut_max
Maximum cutoff for Z-value in the noise.
octaves
Number of octaves for fractal Brownian motion (fBm).
persistence
Amplitude scaling factor between noise octaves.
lacunarity
Frequency scaling factor between noise octaves.
p_ctrl_param
Optional array for control parameters, can modify the Z cutoff dynamically.
p_noise_x
Optional array for X-axis noise perturbation.
p_noise_y
Optional array for Y-axis noise perturbation.
bbox
Bounding box for mapping grid coordinates to world space.
A 2D array containing the generated GavoroNoise values.
Taken from https://www.shadertoy.com/view/MtGcWh
Only available if OpenCL is enabled.
This function leverages an OpenCL kernel to compute the GavoroNoise values on the GPU, allowing for efficient large-scale generation. The kernel applies a combination of fractal Brownian motion (fBm), directional erosion, and other procedural techniques to generate intricate noise patterns.
The optional p_ctrl_param, p_noise_x, and p_noise_y buffers provide additional flexibility for dynamically adjusting noise parameters and perturbations.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, glm::vec2 kw, uint seed, float angle=0.f, float amplitude=0.05f, float angle_spread_ratio=1.f, glm::vec2 kw_multiplier={4.f, 4.f}, float slope_strength=1.f, float branch_strength=2.f, float z_cut_min=0.2f, float z_cut_max=1.f, int octaves=8, float persistence=0.4f, float lacunarity=2.f, const Array *p_ctrl_param=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})

briefdescription.para: Generates an island heightmap from a land mask using radial profiles, slope shaping, optional noise, and water attenuation.

detaileddescription.para: Applies elevation shaping inside the land mask using distance-based profiles, slope controls, optional radial and slope noise, and water-depth modeling.

Optional radial noise field (same size as mask).
Maximum elevation at island center.
Enable smoothing of the distance transform.
Radius of the distance filter.

Maximum slope.
Start of slope ramp (0–1).
End of slope ramp (0–1).
Intensity of slope noise modulation.
Smoothing factor for the final profile.
Intensity of radial displacement noise.
Exponent for radial falloff.
Transition sharpness to water level.
Water depth below the shoreline.
Angle for lee-side (wind shadow) erosion.
Amplitude of lee-side erosion.
Amplitude of uplift (orographic) effect.
Optional output: per-pixel water depth.
Optional output: mask of inland pixels.

simplesect.para: Heightmap array representing the generated island.

memberdef.name: mountain_cone

briefdescription.para: Generates a procedural "mountain cone" heightmap using fractal noise and Voronoi patterns.

detaileddescription.para: This function synthesizes a terrain-like array shaped as a cone with noise-based ridges and surface roughness. The generation combines a cone-shaped envelope (cone_sigmoid), a fractal base noise (gpu::noise_fbm), and a Voronoi ridge structure (voronoi_fbm), producing a mountain with controlled smoothness, angular distortion, and noise-based displacements.

parameterdescription.para: The output array dimensions (width, height).
The random seed for noise generation.
Global scaling factor for the mountain size.
Number of fractal noise octaves for both FBM and Voronoi.
Controls the base frequency (inverse wavelength) of the peak noise.
Controls the amplitude decay across octaves (higher = rougher surface).
Direction (in degrees) for the displacement noise distortion.
Smoothing factor applied in Voronoi blending.
Gamma correction exponent applied at the end.
Controls the cone envelope steepness (higher = sharper peak).
Controls the amplitude of ridge enhancement in the Voronoi term.
Amplitude multiplier for the displacement noise.
The 2D position (in normalized coordinates) of the mountain cone center.
Optional pointer to an external X displacement noise field (can be nullptr).
Optional pointer to an external Y displacement noise field (can be nullptr).
The bounding box (min_x, min_y, max_x, max_y) defining the spatial extent of the map.

simplesect.para: An Array representing the final terrain heightmap in normalized [0, 1] range.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, uint seed, float scale=1.f, int octaves=8, float rugosity=0.2f, float angle=45.f, float gamma=1.1f, bool round_shape=false, bool add_deposition=true, float bulk_amp=0.2f, float base_noise_amp=0.2f, float k_smoothing=0.1f, glm::vec2 center={0.5f, 0.5f}, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: mountain_inselberg

briefdescription.para: Generates a synthetic mountain-like inselberg (isolated hill) heightmap.

detaileddescription.para: This function procedurally creates a 2D elevation field resembling an inselberg, using a combination of fractal noise (FBM), Gaussian pulses, and Voronoi-based structures. It allows control over scale, shape, orientation, rugosity, and optional geological-like effects such as bulk uplift and deposition smoothing.

parameterdescription.para: Output array shape (resolution in x and y).
Random seed used for noise and Voronoi generation.
Global scaling factor for the inselberg width and structure.
Controls roughness of the fractal noise (higher = more irregular).
Orientation angle (degrees) for directional displacements.
Gamma correction factor applied to the final heightmap.
If true, generates a symmetric round shape; if false, adds directional displacement.
If true, applies a smoothing fill step simulating sediment deposition.
Amplitude of bulk uplift applied to the base pulse (0 = none, >0 = raises and normalizes the feature).
Amplitude of the base displacement noise.
Voronoi smoothing parameter (controls ridge sharpness).
Center of the inselberg in normalized coordinates.
Optional pointer to external displacement noise field (X-axis).
Optional pointer to external displacement noise field (Y-axis).
Bounding box of the generation domain in normalized coordinates.

simplesect.para: Array containing the generated inselberg heightmap.

detaileddescription.para: Result**

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, glm::vec2 kw, uint seed, float half_width=0.2f, float angle_spread_ratio=0.5f, float core_size_ratio=1.f, glm::vec2 center={0.5f, 0.5f}, int octaves=8, float weight=0.7f, float persistence=0.5f, float lacunarity=2.f, const Array *p_ctrl_param=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, const Array *p_angle=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: mountain_range_radial

briefdescription.para: Generates a heightmap representing a radial mountain range.

detaileddescription.para: This function creates a heightmap that simulates a mountain range emanating radially from a specified center. The mountain range is influenced by various noise parameters and control attributes.

parameterdescription.para: The dimensions of the output heightmap as a 2D vector.
The wave numbers (frequency components) as a 2D vector.
The seed for random noise generation.
The half-width of the radial mountain range, controlling its spread. Default is 0.2f.
The ratio controlling the angular spread of the mountain range. Default is 0.5f.
The center point of the radial mountain range as normalized coordinates within [0, 1]. Default is {0.5f, 0.5f}.
The number of octaves for fractal noise generation. Default is 8.
The initial weight for noise contribution. Default is 0.7f.
The amplitude scaling factor for subsequent noise octaves. Default is 0.5f.
The frequency scaling factor for subsequent noise octaves. Default is 2.0f.
Optional pointer to an array of control parameters influencing the terrain generation.
Optional pointer to a precomputed noise array for the X-axis.
Optional pointer to a precomputed noise array for the Y-axis.
Optional pointer to an array to output the angle.
The bounding box of the output heightmap in normalized coordinates [xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax]. Default is {0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f, 1.0f}.

simplesect.para: Array The generated heightmap representing the radial mountain range.
Only available if OpenCL is enabled.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, uint seed, float scale=1.f, int octaves=8, float peak_kw=6.f, float rugosity=0.f, float angle=45.f, float k_smoothing=0.f, float gamma=0.25f, bool add_deposition=true, float ridge_amp=0.75f, float base_noise_amp=0.1f, glm::vec2 center={0.5f, 0.5f}, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: mountain_stump

briefdescription.para: Generates a mountain-like heightmap with a flattened (stump-shaped) peak.

detaileddescription.para: This function procedurally creates a terrain resembling a broad mountain with a smooth, truncated summit. The result combines layered fractal noise (FBM), Gaussian shaping, and Voronoi-based ridge formation to produce natural-looking geomorphological features. The shape can be modulated by directional displacement noise, smoothed using deposition simulation, and refined through gamma and smoothing parameters.

parameterdescription.para: Dimensions of the output heightmap (width, height).
Random seed for noise generation.
Global scale factor controlling the size of features.
Number of noise octaves used in FBM generation.
Base spatial frequency of the main ridge/peak structure.
Controls the roughness of base noise displacements.
Orientation angle (in degrees) of the main ridge direction.
Smoothing coefficient applied to the Voronoi FBM ridges.
Gamma correction applied to the ridge intensity.
If true, applies an additional smoothing pass to simulate sediment deposition.
Amplitude scaling factor for ridge prominence.
Amplitude scaling factor for base displacement noise.
Normalized coordinates of the mountain’s center (default domain: [0, 1]²).
Optional pointer to horizontal displacement noise (nullptr for none).
Optional pointer to vertical displacement noise (nullptr for none).
Bounding box of the generated region (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax).

simplesect.para: A 2D Array containing the generated mountain stump heightmap.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, uint seed, float scale=1.f, int octaves=8, float peak_kw=20.f, float rugosity=0.f, float angle=30.f, float angle_spread_ratio=0.25f, float gamma=1.f, bool add_deposition=true, float bulk_amp=1.f, float base_noise_amp=0.1f, glm::vec2 center={0.5f, 0.5f}, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: mountain_tibesti

briefdescription.para: Generates a synthetic "Tibesti" mountain heightmap.

detaileddescription.para: This function procedurally creates a 2D elevation field inspired by the Tibesti mountains, a desert volcanic range. The terrain is built by combining Gabor wavelets, fractal simplex noise, and a Gaussian envelope, producing sharp volcanic peaks modulated by erosion-like patterns.

parameterdescription.para: Output array shape (resolution in x and y).
Random seed used for noise and wavelet generation.
Global scaling factor controlling the overall mountain size.
Number of octaves used in the fractal noise and Gabor FBM.
Base frequency of the peak features (scaled internally by scale).
Controls roughness of the fractal noise (higher = more irregular).
Orientation angle (degrees) for Gabor waves.
Spread ratio of the Gabor wave orientation (controls anisotropy).
Gamma correction factor applied to auxiliary noise fields.
If true, applies a smoothing fill step simulating sediment deposition.
Amplitude of bulk uplift applied to the peaks (affects normalization with noise weighting).
Amplitude of the base displacement noise.
Center of the mountain in normalized coordinates.
Optional pointer to external displacement noise field (X-axis).
Optional pointer to external displacement noise field (Y-axis).
Bounding box of the generation domain in normalized coordinates.

simplesect.para: Array containing the generated Tibesti mountain heightmap.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, float angle, uint seed, glm::vec2 kw={2.f, 2.f}, float noise_amp=0.1f, float noise_rugosity=0.f, bool noise_inflate=true, float r=1.2f, int nsteps=8, float elevation_exponent=0.7f, float shape_gain=4.f, float scale=0.5f, float outer_slope=0.1f, const Array *p_ctrl_param=nullptr, const glm::vec2 &center={0.5f, 0.5f}, const glm::vec4 &bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: multisteps

briefdescription.para: GPU-accelerated multi-step height generation with procedural noise.

detaileddescription.para: Builds the same multi-step profile as the CPU version but injects a Voronoi-FBM noise field to modulate the step positions. Noise can be inflated or deflated and is projected onto the step axis before being passed to the CPU multisteps() implementation.

parameterdescription.para: Output array resolution.
Rotation angle in degrees.
Noise seed.
Base wave numbers for noise.
Amplitude of injected noise.
Roughness of the FBM noise.
Whether to invert noise direction.
Geometric ratio between steps.
Number of steps.
Exponent shaping step heights.
Gain used to reshape intra-step transitions.

Base wavelength/frequency.

Projection direction.

memberdef.name: polygon_field

briefdescription.para: Generates a scalar field representing the signed distance to randomly generated polygons.

detaileddescription.para: This function procedurally generates a field where each pixel stores the signed distance to the nearest polygon, using a smooth signed distance function (SDF) with optional clamping. The polygons are generated randomly within the specified bounding box, with configurable vertex counts, sizes, jitter, and density. Optionally, displacement noise fields can be applied to polygon positions.

parameterdescription.para: Resolution of the output field (width, height).
Scaling factor for field coordinates (world units per pixel).
Random seed for polygon generation.
Minimum polygon radius (relative to bbox size).
Maximum polygon radius (relative to bbox size).
Distance threshold for clamping the SDF value (used to soften edges).
Smoothness parameter for clamping.
Minimum number of vertices per polygon.
Maximum number of vertices per polygon.
Fraction of pixels covered by polygons (approximate).
Random displacement factor applied to polygon vertices.
Random position shift applied to polygon center.
Optional displacement noise field in the X direction (nullptr to disable).
Optional displacement noise field in the Y direction (nullptr to disable).
Bounding box in normalized coordinates {xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax}.

simplesect.para: Array 2D array containing the signed distance field.
Polygons are randomly generated per call and are not guaranteed to be convex.
The sign of the SDF is negative inside polygons and positive outside.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, glm::vec2 kw, uint seed, float rmin=0.05f, float rmax=0.8f, float clamping_dist=0.1f, float clamping_k=0.1f, int n_vertices_min=3, int n_vertices_max=16, float density=0.1f, glm::vec2 jitter={0.5f, 0.5f}, float shift=0.1f, int octaves=8, float persistence=0.5f, float lacunarity=2.f, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_distance=nullptr, const Array *p_density_multiplier=nullptr, const Array *p_size_multiplier=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: polygon_field_fbm

briefdescription.para: Generates a scalar field representing the signed distance to randomly generated polygons combined with fractal Brownian motion (fBm) noise modulation.

detaileddescription.para: Similar to polygon_field(), but the generated SDF is modulated by an fBm noise function to create more natural, irregular shapes. The fBm parameters allow control over the noise persistence, lacunarity, and number of octaves.

parameterdescription.para: Resolution of the output field (width, height).
Scaling factor for field coordinates (world units per pixel).
Random seed for polygon generation and fBm noise.
Minimum polygon radius (relative to bbox size).
Maximum polygon radius (relative to bbox size).
Distance threshold for clamping the SDF value (used to soften edges).
Smoothness parameter for clamping.
Minimum number of vertices per polygon.
Maximum number of vertices per polygon.
Fraction of pixels covered by polygons (approximate).
Random displacement factor applied to polygon vertices.
Random position shift applied to polygon center.
Number of fBm octaves.
Amplitude decay per octave in fBm.
Frequency multiplier per octave in fBm.
Optional displacement noise field in the X direction (nullptr to disable).
Optional displacement noise field in the Y direction (nullptr to disable).
Bounding box in normalized coordinates {xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax}.

simplesect.para: Array 2D array containing the signed distance field modulated by fBm.
The sign of the SDF is negative inside polygons and positive outside.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, uint seed, float scale=1.f, int octaves=8, float peak_kw=4.f, float rugosity=0.f, float angle=30.f, float gamma=1.f, bool add_deposition=true, float bulk_amp=0.3f, float base_noise_amp=0.1f, float k_smoothing=0.f, glm::vec2 center={0.5f, 0.5f}, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: shattered_peak

briefdescription.para: Generates a synthetic "shattered peak" terrain heightmap.

detaileddescription.para: This function procedurally creates a 2D elevation field resembling a sharp, fractured mountain peak. It combines a Gaussian pulse envelope, fractal noise displacements, and Voronoi-based primitives with edge-distance shaping. The result is a rugged peak structure with broken ridges and steep slopes.

parameterdescription.para: Output array shape (resolution in x and y).
Random seed used for noise and Voronoi generation.
Global scaling factor controlling the overall peak size.
Base frequency of the peak features (scaled internally by scale).
Controls roughness of the fractal noise (higher = more irregular).
Orientation angle (degrees) for directional displacements.
Gamma correction factor applied to the final heightmap.
If true, applies a smoothing fill step simulating sediment deposition.
Amplitude of bulk uplift applied to the peak (internally overridden to 0.5f for normalization).
Amplitude of the base displacement noise.
Voronoi smoothing parameter (controls ridge sharpness).
Center of the peak in normalized coordinates.
Optional pointer to external displacement noise field (X-axis).
Optional pointer to external displacement noise field (Y-axis).
Bounding box of the generation domain in normalized coordinates.

simplesect.para: Array containing the generated shattered peak heightmap.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, float density, uint seed, float k_smoothing=0.f, float exp_sigma=0.f, float alpha=0.f, float alpha_span=M_PI, VoronoiReturnType return_type=VoronoiReturnType::F1_SQUARED, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f}, glm::vec4 bbox_points={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: vorolines

briefdescription.para: Generates a Voronoi-based pattern where cells are defined by proximity to random lines.

detaileddescription.para: This function generates an OpenCL-accelerated Voronoi-like pattern based on the distance from each pixel to a set of randomly oriented lines. Each line is defined by a random point and a direction sampled from a uniform distribution around a given angle.

parameterdescription.para: The resolution of the resulting 2D array (width, height).
Number of base points per unit area used to define lines.
Seed for the random number generator used to generate base points and directions.
Kernel smoothing factor; controls how sharp or soft the distance fields are.
Exponential smoothing parameter applied to the computed distance field.
Base angle (in radians) used to orient the generated lines.
Maximum angular deviation from alpha; controls line orientation variability.
Type of Voronoi output to return (e.g., F1, F2, edge distance, smoothed field, etc.).
Optional pointer to an input noise field applied to the X coordinates (can be nullptr).
Optional pointer to an input noise field applied to the Y coordinates (can be nullptr).
Bounding box in normalized coordinates (min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y) of the final array.
Bounding box within which random base points are sampled.

simplesect.para: A 2D array (of type Array) containing the computed distance field based on line proximity.
Each line is defined from a point (x, y) to a direction offset using angle theta = alpha + rand * alpha_span.
The OpenCL kernel "vorolines" must be defined and compiled beforehand.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, float density, uint seed, float k_smoothing=0.f, float exp_sigma=0.f, float alpha=0.f, float alpha_span=M_PI, VoronoiReturnType return_type=VoronoiReturnType::F1_SQUARED, int octaves=8, float weight=0.7f, float persistence=0.5f, float lacunarity=2.f, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f}, glm::vec4 bbox_points={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: vorolines_fbm

briefdescription.para: Generates a Voronoi-based pattern using distances to lines defined by random points and angles, with additional fractal Brownian motion (fBm) noise modulation.

detaileddescription.para: This function extends the standard vorolines generation by introducing fBm-based warping of the coordinate space, resulting in more organic and fractal-like structures. It creates a Voronoi distance field based on proximity to oriented line segments and distorts the result using multi-octave procedural noise.

parameterdescription.para: Output resolution of the 2D array (width, height).
Number of base points per unit area used to define lines.
Seed value for the random number generator.
Kernel smoothing coefficient to soften distance values (e.g., for blending).
Sigma value for optional exponential smoothing on the final field.
Base orientation angle (in radians) of lines generated from random points.
Maximum angle deviation from alpha, determining directional randomness of lines.
Type of output to return (e.g., F1, F2, distance to edge, smoothed version).
Number of noise octaves used in the fBm modulation.
Weight of each octave's contribution to the total noise.
Amplitude decay factor for each successive octave (commonly 0.5–0.8).
Frequency multiplier for each successive octave (commonly 2.0).
Optional pointer to an external noise field applied to X coordinates (can be nullptr).
Optional pointer to an external noise field applied to Y coordinates (can be nullptr).
Bounding box for the final image domain (min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y).
Bounding box from which the initial set of points are sampled.

simplesect.para: A 2D Array representing the Voronoi-fBm field, distorted by noise and influenced by distance to random lines.
This version uses internally computed fBm noise unless external fields (p_noise_x, p_noise_y) are provided.
This function requires an OpenCL kernel named "vorolines_fbm" to be compiled and accessible.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, glm::vec2 kw, uint seed, glm::vec2 jitter={0.5f, 0.5f}, float k_smoothing=0.f, float exp_sigma=0.f, VoronoiReturnType return_type=VoronoiReturnType::F1_SQUARED, const Array *p_ctrl_param=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: voronoi

briefdescription.para: Generates a Voronoi diagram in a 2D array with configurable properties.

parameterdescription.para: The dimensions of the output array as a 2D vector of integers.
The frequency scale factors for the Voronoi cells, given as a 2D vector of floats.
A seed value for random number generation, ensuring reproducibility.
(Optional) The amount of random variation in the positions of Voronoi cell sites, given as a 2D vector of floats. Defaults to {0.5f, 0.5f}.
(Optional) The type of value to compute for the Voronoi diagram. Defaults to VoronoiReturnType::F1_SQUARED.
(Optional) A pointer to an Array used to control the Voronoi computation. Used here as a multiplier for the jitter. If nullptr, no control is applied.
(Optional) A pointer to an Array providing additional noise in the x-direction for cell positions. If nullptr, no x-noise is applied.
(Optional) A pointer to an Array providing additional noise in the y-direction for cell positions. If nullptr, no y-noise is applied.
(Optional) The bounding box for the Voronoi computation, given as a 4D vector of floats representing {min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y}. Defaults to {0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f}.

simplesect.para: A 2D array representing the generated Voronoi diagram.
Only available if OpenCL is enabled.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, glm::vec2 kw, uint seed, glm::vec2 jitter={0.5f, 0.5f}, float k_smoothing=0.f, float exp_sigma=0.f, VoronoiReturnType return_type=VoronoiReturnType::F1_SQUARED, int octaves=8, float weight=0.7f, float persistence=0.5f, float lacunarity=2.f, const Array *p_ctrl_param=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: voronoi_fbm

briefdescription.para: Generates a Voronoi diagram in a 2D array with configurable properties.

parameterdescription.para: The dimensions of the output array as a 2D vector of integers.
The frequency scale factors for the base Voronoi cells, given as a 2D vector of floats.
A seed value for random number generation, ensuring reproducibility.
(Optional) The amount of random variation in the positions of Voronoi cell sites, given as a 2D vector of floats. Defaults to {0.5f, 0.5f}.
(Optional) The type of value to compute for the Voronoi diagram. Defaults to VoronoiReturnType::F1_SQUARED.
(Optional) The number of layers (octaves) in the fractal Brownian motion. Defaults to 8.
(Optional) The initial weight of the base layer in the FBM computation. Defaults to 0.7f.
(Optional) The persistence factor that controls the amplitude reduction between octaves. Defaults to 0.5f.
(Optional) The lacunarity factor that controls the frequency increase between octaves. Defaults to 2.f.
(Optional) A pointer to an Array used to control the Voronoi computation. If nullptr, no control is applied.
(Optional) A pointer to an Array providing additional noise in the x-direction for cell positions. If nullptr, no x-noise is applied.
(Optional) A pointer to an Array providing additional noise in the y-direction for cell positions. If nullptr, no y-noise is applied.
(Optional) The bounding box for the Voronoi computation, given as a 4D vector of floats representing {min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y}. Defaults to {0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f}.

simplesect.para: A 2D array representing the generated Voronoi diagram.
Only available if OpenCL is enabled.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, glm::vec2 kw, uint seed, glm::vec2 jitter={0.5f, 0.5f}, const Array *p_ctrl_param=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: voronoi_edge_distance

briefdescription.para: Computes the Voronoi edge distance.

parameterdescription.para: The shape of the grid as a 2D vector (width, height).
The weights for the Voronoi kernel as a 2D vector.
The random seed used for generating Voronoi points.
Optional parameter for controlling jitter in Voronoi point placement (default is {0.5f, 0.5f}).
Optional pointer to an Array specifying control parameters for Voronoi grid jitter (default is nullptr).
Reference to the input noise arrays.

memberdef.name: voronoise

briefdescription.para: Generates a 2D Voronoi noise array.

detaileddescription.para: This function computes a Voronoi noise pattern based on the input parameters and returns it as a 2D array. The noise is calculated in the OpenCL kernel noise_voronoise, which uses a combination of hashing and smoothstep functions to generate a weighted Voronoi noise field.

simplesect.para: Taken from https://www.shadertoy.com/view/Xd23Dh
Only available if OpenCL is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: The dimensions of the 2D output array as a vector (width and height).
Wave numbers for scaling the noise pattern, represented as a 2D vector.
A control parameter for the noise, adjusting the contribution of random offsets.
A control parameter for the noise, affecting the smoothness of the pattern.
Optional pointer to an Array specifying control parameters for Voronoi grid jitter (default is nullptr).
Reference to the input noise arrays.
A seed value for random number generation, ensuring reproducibility.

simplesect.para: An Array object containing the generated 2D Voronoi noise values.

Noise wavenumbers {kx, ky} for each directions.
Random seed number.
Number of octaves.
Octave weighting.
Octave persistence.
Defines the wavenumber ratio between each octaves.
Reference to the control parameter array (acts as a multiplier for the weight parameter).
Reference to the input noise arrays.

Taken from https://www.shadertoy.com/view/clGyWm
Only available if OpenCL is enabled.

memberdef.name: vororand

briefdescription.para: Generates a 2D Voronoi-based scalar field using OpenCL.

detaileddescription.para: This function computes a Voronoi diagram or derived metric (such as F1, F2, or edge distances) on a grid of given shape. A set of random points is generated within an extended bounding box, based on the desired density and variability, to reduce edge artifacts. Optionally, per-pixel displacement can be applied through noise fields.
The result is stored in an Array object representing a 2D scalar field.

parameterdescription.para: Dimensions of the output array (width x height).
Number of random points per unit area for Voronoi diagram.
Amount of randomness added to the point generation bounding box.
Seed for random number generation used in point sampling.
Smoothing factor used in soft minimum/maximum Voronoi distance computations.
Standard deviation used in exponential falloff for edge distance computation.
Type of Voronoi computation to perform (e.g., F1, F2, F2-F1, edge distance).
Optional pointer to a noise field applied to X coordinates of grid points.
Optional pointer to a noise field applied to Y coordinates of grid points.
Bounding box of the domain in which the field is computed: {xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax}.
Bounding box for point generation, usually larger than bbox to avoid edge effects.

simplesect.para: An Array object containing the computed scalar field.

listitem.para: The kernel "vororand" must be compiled and available in the OpenCL context.
If p_noise_x or p_noise_y are provided, they must match the shape of the output array.
The generated point cloud will be larger than bbox to reduce border artifacts.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, const std::vector< float > &xp, const std::vector< float > &yp, float k_smoothing=0.f, float exp_sigma=0.f, VoronoiReturnType return_type=VoronoiReturnType::F1_SQUARED, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: vororand

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, glm::vec2 kw, uint seed, float kw_multiplier=2.f, float vorticity=0.f, float density=1.f, int octaves=8, float weight=0.7f, float persistence=0.5f, float lacunarity=2.f, const Array *p_ctrl_param=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_x=nullptr, const Array *p_noise_y=nullptr, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: wavelet_noise

briefdescription.para: Generates 2D wavelet noise using an OpenCL kernel.

detaileddescription.para: This function creates a 2D noise field of the given shape using multi-octave wavelet turbulence. The noise is computed on the GPU via the "wavelet_noise" OpenCL kernel. Various parameters control the frequency evolution, amplitude decay, vorticity injection, and optional modulation using additional input arrays.

parameterdescription.para: Resolution of the output noise array (width, height).
Base wave number (frequency) in each axis. Controls the initial spatial frequency of the wavelet field.
Integer seed used by the random hashing functions inside the kernel.
Multiplier applied to the wave number per octave (similar to gain). Higher values increase frequency growth across octaves.
Amount of rotational distortion injected into the noise. When > 0, the kernel applies additional angular perturbations to produce turbulent flow-like patterns.
Global scaling factor controlling the overall contrast or amplitude of the generated noise.
Number of wavelet noise layers combined. Higher octaves yield more detail but increase computational cost.
Weight applied to the base octave, used as a global intensity multiplier before persistence attenuation is applied.
Amplitude decay factor per octave. Values in (0,1) produce the classic fractal noise falloff; higher values retain more high-frequency detail.
Frequency multiplier per octave. Controls how rapidly frequency increases at each octave, with typical values in the range [1.5, 3.0].
Optional pointer to a control-parameter array. When provided, values inside this array modulate the generated noise spatially. Pass nullptr to disable this feature.
Optional pointer to an auxiliary noise array used to perturb sampling positions horizontally. Pass nullptr to disable.
Optional pointer to an auxiliary noise array used to perturb sampling positions vertically. Pass nullptr to disable.
Bounding-box mapping (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). Converts pixel-space indices into world-space coordinates for spatially consistent noise.

simplesect.para: Array A 2D floating-point array containing the generated wavelet noise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &array1, const Array &array2, float k=0.2f)

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &z, int ir_gradient=1, float gradient_weight=1.f, float gradient_scaling_factor=0.f, float flow_weight=0.05f, float talus_ref=0.f, float clipping_ratio=50.f, float flow_gamma=1.f, float k_smooth=0.01f)
memberdef.name: select_soil_flow

briefdescription.para: Computes a soil–flow selection map based on terrain gradient, river mask, and smoothing parameters.

parameterdescription.para: Input heightmap.
Radius used for the morphological gradient.
Weight applied to the gradient magnitude.
Scaling factor applied to the gradient; defaults to z.shape.x if <= 0.
Reference talus value for river extraction; defaults to 1.f / z.shape.x if <= 0.
Clipping ratio used in the river selection step.
Gamma correction exponent applied to the river mask; 1.0 disables correction.
Smoothing parameter for the maximum blending.

simplesect.para: A 2D Array representing the final soil-flow selection mask.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &z, int ir_max=64, int ir_min=0, int steps=4, float smaller_scales_weight=1.f, ClampMode curvature_clamp_mode=ClampMode::POSITIVE_ONLY, float curvature_clamping=1.f)
memberdef.name: select_soil_rocks

briefdescription.para: Computes a multi-scale soil/rock selector using curvature analysis.

detaileddescription.para: Applies progressively increasing smoothing radii in logarithmic steps (handling ir_min = 0), computes the mean curvature at each scale, clamps it, and accumulates the maximum response across all scales.

parameterdescription.para: Input heightmap or scalar field.
Maximum smoothing radius (log-scale upper bound).
Minimum smoothing radius (0 allowed; replaced by 1 for log scale).
Number of logarithmic scales to evaluate.
Weight factor applied when descending scales.
Mode used to clamp curvature values.
Curvature clamp threshold.

simplesect.para: An Array representing soil/rock selection values across scales.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &z, int ir_curvature=0, int ir_gradient=4, ClampMode curvature_clamp_mode=ClampMode::POSITIVE_ONLY, float curvature_clamping=10.f, float curvature_weight=1.f, float gradient_weight=1.f, float gradient_scaling_factor=0.f)
memberdef.name: select_soil_weathered

briefdescription.para: Computes a soil weathering selection map based on curvature and gradient analysis.

detaileddescription.para:
This function generates a combined array representing the degree of soil weathering using two main components:
Curvature contribution: derived from smoothed height variations.
Gradient contribution: derived from local slope magnitudes.

parameterdescription.para: Input heightmap or scalar field.
Radius (in pixels or samples) for curvature smoothing. If zero, curvature smoothing is skipped.
Radius (in pixels or samples) for gradient computation.
Clamping mode applied to curvature values (see ClampMode).
Maximum absolute value used for curvature clamping.
Weight applied to the curvature component in the final mix.
Weight applied to the gradient component in the final mix.
Empirical scaling factor for both curvature and gradient terms. If non-positive, defaults to z.shape.x.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &z, const Array &gradient_norm, int ir_curvature, ClampMode curvature_clamp_mode, float curvature_clamping, float curvature_weight, float gradient_weight, float gradient_scaling_factor)
memberdef.name: select_soil_weathered

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &z, int ir, bool ridge_select=false)

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &z, const Array &advected_field, int iterations, int nparticles, uint seed, bool reverse=false, bool post_filter=true, float post_filter_sigma=0.125f, float advection_length=0.1f, float value_persistence=0.99f, float inertia=0.f, const Array *p_advection_mask=nullptr, const Array *p_mask=nullptr)
memberdef.name: advection_particle

briefdescription.para: Performs particle-based advection on a scalar field.

detaileddescription.para: This function advects particles through a velocity field (or gradient field) derived from the input array z. Each particle carries a value from the advected_field and evolves over a number of iterations according to advection parameters such as inertia and persistence. Optionally, advection and masking fields can be applied to control which areas are affected.

parameterdescription.para: Input scalar field defining the base flow or gradient field used for particle advection.
The scalar field whose values are transported along particle trajectories.
Number of advection iterations to perform per particle.
Number of particles to simulate during advection.
Random seed used for initializing particle positions and stochastic behavior.
If true, reverses the advection direction (useful for backtracing or inverse advection).
If true, applies a smoothing or filtering operation after advection to reduce artifacts.
Maximum advection step length per iteration (controls particle displacement scale).
Controls how much of a particle’s advected value is preserved between iterations (1.0 means full preservation, lower values introduce decay).
Controls how much a particle’s previous velocity affects its next step (simulates momentum).
Optional mask array controlling where advection occurs (nullptr to disable).
Optional general mask restricting where output values are written (nullptr to disable).

simplesect.para: An Array representing the advected version of advected_field after applying particle advection.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &z, const Array &advected_field, int nparticles, uint seed, bool reverse=false, bool post_filter=true, float post_filter_sigma=0.125f, float advection_length=0.1f, float value_persistence=0.99f, float inertia=0.f, const Array *p_advection_mask=nullptr, const Array *p_mask=nullptr)
memberdef.name: advection_particle

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &dx, const Array &dy, const Array &advected_field, int nparticles, uint seed, bool reverse=false, bool post_filter=true, float post_filter_sigma=0.125f, float advection_length=0.1f, float value_persistence=0.99f, float inertia=0.f, const Array *p_advection_mask=nullptr, const Array *p_mask=nullptr)
memberdef.name: advection_particle

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &z, const Array &advected_field, float advection_length=0.1f, float value_persistence=0.9f, const Array *p_mask=nullptr)
memberdef.name: advection_warp

briefdescription.para: Performs 2D field advection based on the gradient of a heightmap using a warp-based technic (simplified approach).

detaileddescription.para: This function computes the X and Y gradients of the given heightmap z and uses them as flow directions to advect the input advected_field. The result is a new field where values have been displaced according to the gradient direction and the specified advection parameters.

parameterdescription.para: The heightmap (or scalar field) used to compute advection directions.
The field to be advected along the gradient of z.
The step length used for advection; larger values produce stronger displacement.
The persistence factor applied to the advected values, typically in the range [0, 1]; controls how much of the original value is preserved.
Optional pointer to a mask array; if provided, advection is applied only where the mask is nonzero.

simplesect.para: A new Array representing the advected version of advected_field.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Array &z, const Array &advected_field, const Array &dx, const Array &dy, float advection_length=0.1f, float value_persistence=0.9f, const Array *p_mask=nullptr)
memberdef.name: advection_warp
